title: NERC Filing Deadline Compliance Countdown

compliance.assurance_rationale: Uplifted from contributing to partial: UC provides meaningful monitoring evidence for CIP-008-6 R4 Part 4.1.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that NERC CIP CIP-008-6 R4 Part 4.1 is enforced — Splunk UC-22.13.41: NERC Filing Deadline Compliance Countdown.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.13.41' running on index=itsm sourcetype=cip008_incident (discovered_at, filing_deadline, filed_at), archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

spl:
index=itsm sourcetype=cip008_incident earliest=-60d
| eval disc=strptime(discovered_at,"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")
| eval due=strptime(filing_deadline,"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")
| eval hours_left=round((due-now())/3600,2)
| eval filed=if(isnotnull(filed_at),1,0)
| where filed=0
| table incident_id, discovered_at, filing_deadline, hours_left, owner
| sort hours_left
description: We compute hours remaining until regulatory filing deadlines from the official incident discovery time to reduce missed submissions.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for NERC CIP compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes itsm and sourcetypes cip008_incident, cip008_incident`. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Populate deadlines per NERC filing category from legal. (2) Alert at seventy-two, twenty-four, and six hours. (3) Attach filing confirmation artifact. (4) Restrict access to sensitive fields. Indexes required: `index=itsm`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=cip008_incident`, `sourcetype=cip008_incident``.
visualization: Single value (min hours left), Table (open filings), Timeline. Key fields: `disc`, `discovered_at`, `due`, `filed`, `filing_deadline`, `hours_left`.
knownFalsePositives: Planned maintenance, registered change tickets, and approved work can look like noncompliance in raw telemetry; confirm against the current change record before you escalate.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise Security (263).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=itsm` `sourcetype=cip008_incident` (discovered_at, filing_deadline, filed_at).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Populate deadlines per NERC filing category from legal. (2) Alert at seventy-two, twenty-four, and six hours. (3) Attach filing confirmation artifact. (4) Restrict access to sensitive fields.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=itsm sourcetype=cip008_incident earliest=-60d
| eval disc=strptime(discovered_at,"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")
| eval due=strptime(filing_deadline,"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")
| eval hours_left=round((due-now())/3600,2)
| eval filed=if(isnotnull(filed_at),1,0)
| where filed=0
| table incident_id, discovered_at, filing_deadline, hours_left, owner
| sort hours_left
```

Understanding this SPL

**NERC Filing Deadline Compliance Countdown (CIP-008-6 R4 Part 4.1)** — We compute hours remaining until regulatory filing deadlines from the official incident discovery time to reduce missed submissions.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=itsm` `sourcetype=cip008_incident` (discovered_at, filing_deadline, filed_at). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise Security (263). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: itsm; **sourcetype**: cip008_incident. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=itsm, sourcetype=cip008_incident, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **disc** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **due** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **hours_left** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **filed** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Filters the current rows with `where filed=0` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Output fields: disc, discovered_at, due, filed, filing_deadline, hours_left, incident_id, owner.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the ESP firewall log, Windows Security, or the asset register—whichever the search is built to mirror. Spot-check a few source events, reconcile counts, and verify field extractions, thresholds, and index permissions for the people who need this evidence.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Single value (min hours left), Table (open filings), Timeline
grandmaExplanation: We compute hours remaining until regulatory filing deadlines from the official incident discovery time to reduce missed submissions. That is important for the big power grid, where proof needs to line up with what the rules expect.
